findings.message: The empty-TOC managed-region byte form '<!-- toc -->\n\n<!-- /toc -->' (one blank line, no bullets) is never byte-asserted. It is only exercised by test_fixed_point, which proves render is a fixed point but cannot detect wrong empty-region spacing: a variant emitting two blank lines is still a fixed point and would pass every existing test. test_build_toc_empty_* only assert build_toc()=='', not how render splices an empty body. The populated form is byte-locked by simple.expected.md; the empty branch (only-leading-H1 doc, or --max-depth below all headings) is unguarded at the render level. This is plan verification check (g)'s empty-TOC-collapse clause.
findings.remediation:
Add a golden-byte assertion for the empty branch, e.g. render('# T

<!-- toc -->
<!-- /toc -->
', 6) == '# T

<!-- toc -->

<!-- /toc -->
', plus the --max-depth-1-with-leading-H1 collapse case. A small data/empty_toc.expected.md keeps the whitespace contract reviewable.

findings.message: Defense-in-depth (not exploitable in this local single-user CLI): (1) path.write_text truncates-then-writes, so an interrupted run (crash, SIGKILL, ENOSPC) can leave the user's Markdown file truncated with no backup; (2) reads/writes follow symlinks and there is a benign read->write TOCTOU gap. No network or privilege boundary is crossed; heading text echoed into link text is correct for a Markdown transform with no shell/SQL/template sink; regexes (ATX_RE/FENCE_RE/IDCOUNT_RE/slugify) were timed and scale linearly (no ReDoS); runtime dependencies are empty and dev deps carry pinned floors.
findings.remediation: Write to a temp file in the target directory and os.replace() it over the target for an atomic rename, eliminating the truncation/data-loss window. Optionally realpath the target so the temp file lands on the same filesystem. No behavior change required for the current threat model.
